SFP Help
Overview
Settings
General Notifications Email Templates Catalog Material Types Audiences Statuses Users Groups Backups
SFP Settings Guide

Settings Guide

How to configure SFP — messages patrons see, catalog connections, statuses, users, and more.

Overview

Settings are only accessible to Admin users. You get there from the navigation bar — look for your name or avatar in the top right, or go directly via Settings in the nav. The sidebar inside Settings lets you jump between these areas: General, Notifications (with General, Staff Email, and Default Patron Email tabs), Email Templates, Catalog, Material Types, Audiences, Statuses, Users, Groups, and Backups.

Most changes take effect immediately after saving. A few (like messaging changes) are visible to patrons as soon as you save.

General

Controls patron PIN login, request limits, ILL messaging, and the messages patrons see at key moments during submission.

Patron

SettingWhat it does
Enable Patron PIN LoginWhen on, a "Sign in with your library card PIN" button appears on the submission form. Patrons can authenticate with their barcode and PIN to view their submitted requests and statuses at /my-requests.

Request Limits

Controls how many requests a patron can submit and how the limit window is measured.

SettingWhat it does
Request Limit CountHow many requests a patron can submit within the active window. Default: 5.
Limit Window Type How the window is measured. Choose one of three modes:
  • Rolling — counts requests within a sliding day window (e.g. any 30-day period). Configure the window length below.
  • Calendar Month — resets on the same day every month (e.g. the 1st). Configure the reset day below.
  • Calendar Week — resets every Monday.
Rolling Window LengthOnly shown when Rolling is selected. The number of days in the sliding window. Default: 30.
Monthly Reset DayOnly shown when Calendar Month is selected. The day of the month the counter resets (1–28). Default: 1.
💡
When a patron hits the limit, both the submission form and the My Requests view show a message indicating when they can submit again.

ILL (Interlibrary Loan)

SettingWhat it does
ILL Age ThresholdIf the item's publish date is older than this many days, the patron sees the ILL Warning Message during submission. Default: 730 days (2 years).
ILL Warning MessageThe message shown to patrons when their item is older than the threshold. Supports rich text formatting.

Messaging

These are the messages patrons see at various points. All support rich text (bold, links, etc.).

SettingWhen it appears
Submission Success MessageShown after a patron's request is successfully submitted.
Catalog Owned MessageShown when the patron confirms the catalog already has the item — no request is created.
Duplicate Request MessageShown when a different patron has already requested the same title.
Duplicate Self-Request MessageShown when a patron submits something they've already requested themselves.
Auto-Order Author Exclusion MessageShown when a patron submits a future release by an author on the auto-order exclusion list — no request is created.

Auto-Order Author Exclusions

A plain-text list of author names, one per line. When a patron submits a title with a future release date and the author matches this list, SFP shows the exclusion message instead of creating a request. This is useful for high-demand authors whose new titles you always pre-order automatically.

💡
The exclusion only triggers when the item has a specific future release date (like 2025-09-15) — not just a year. Vague dates won't trigger it.

Syndetics

SettingWhat it does
Syndetics Client IDYour library's Syndetics client ID, used to display book cover images during patron submission.

Notifications

Controls all automated emails SFP sends — to selectors when new requests arrive, and to patrons when their request status changes. The Notifications settings page has three tabs: General, Staff Email, and Default Patron Email.

General tab

SettingWhat it does
Enable NotificationsGlobal on/off for all SFP emails. Disables everything without touching individual settings.
Enable Patron Status EmailsTurn off to stop patron emails without disabling staff routing.
Email Preview EnabledWhen on, a preview modal appears every time a status change would send a patron email. Selectors can review the message — and optionally skip it — before it goes out.
Email Editing EnabledOnly available when Email Preview is also on. Allows selectors to edit the To, Subject, CC, BCC, and body before sending. Changes apply to that send only — templates are not modified. This setting is automatically disabled if you turn off Email Preview.
💡
Enable Email Preview without Email Editing if you want selectors to see what will be sent but not change it. Enable both if you want selectors to be able to adjust messaging on a case-by-case basis.

Staff Email tab

Controls routing emails sent to selector groups when a new request is submitted.

SettingWhat it does
Enable Staff Routing EmailsTurn off to stop routing emails without disabling patron notifications.
Staff Routing — SubjectSubject line for routing emails. Supports placeholders like {title}, {author}, {material_type}.
Staff Routing — Email BodyHTML body for routing emails. Leave blank to use the built-in default template.

Default Patron Email tab

A fallback subject and body used when no matching Email Template exists for the triggered status. If you have templates configured for all your notification statuses, this tab is effectively unused.

SettingWhat it does
Patron Status — SubjectFallback subject line for patron status emails. Supports the same placeholders as staff routing.
Patron Status — Email BodyFallback HTML body. Leave blank to use the built-in default template.
💡
Available placeholders: {title}, {author}, {patron_name}, {patron_first_name}, {material_type}, {audience}, {status}, {submitted_date}, {request_url}.
💡
To control which statuses trigger a patron email, edit the status under Settings → Statuses and check Notify Patron.

Email Templates

Email templates let you define per-status patron emails — a different subject, body, and message tone for each notification status. Templates are managed under Settings → Notifications → Emails.

When a patron email is triggered, SFP looks for an enabled template linked to that status (and optionally the request's material type). If a match is found, it's used. If no template matches, the fallback from the Default Patron Email tab is used instead.

Template fields

FieldWhat it does
Template nameInternal label — not shown to patrons. Use something descriptive like "Purchased notification".
EnableUncheck to disable the template without deleting it. Disabled templates are never sent.
SubjectThe email subject line. Supports placeholders like {title} and {patron_first_name}. Use the Insert buttons below the field to add a placeholder without typing it.
Email BodyRich-text editor (Trix). Supports all placeholders. Use the Insert buttons below the editor to add tokens at the cursor position.
Triggered by statusesSelect one or more statuses that send this template. Click Clear selection to deselect all. Only statuses with Notify Patron turned on will actually fire.
Material typesOptional. Leave empty to match all material types, or select specific ones so this template only sends for those. Click Clear selection to remove all material type restrictions.
Default templateOnly one template can be marked default. It is used as a catch-all when a request's status matches no other template (regardless of material type).
💡
Default templates are seeded for On Order, Purchased, Denied, and ILL Referred. Edit them freely — re-running request:seed-default will not overwrite templates that already exist.
💡
If multiple templates match the same status (e.g. one for Books and one for DVDs), SFP sends all matching templates. Use material type restrictions to send targeted messages per format.

Catalog

Controls how SFP connects to your catalog and external metadata sources during patron submission.

Catalog Search

SettingWhat it does
Enable Catalog SearchWhen on, SFP searches the catalog during submission so patrons can be redirected to place a hold if the item is already owned.
Bibliocommons Library SlugYour library's subdomain on Bibliocommons (e.g. dcpl for dcpl.bibliocommons.com).
Catalog Search URL TemplateThe URL pattern used to link patrons to catalog search results. Uses {slug} and {query} as placeholders.

ISBNdb

SettingWhat it does
Enable ISBNdb SearchWhen on, SFP looks up the title in ISBNdb after submission to enrich the record with ISBN, publisher, and publication date. Only runs when the catalog search returns no results.

Post-Submit Processing Mode

ValueBehavior
waitThe patron's browser waits on the page while catalog and ISBNdb lookups run. Fastest feedback for the patron.
emailThe patron sees a confirmation immediately and lookups run in the background. A follow-up email is sent when processing completes.

BiblioCommons Format Labels

Bibliocommons returns raw format codes (like BK or MUSIC_CD) in catalog results. This table lets you map those codes to friendlier display labels shown to patrons (e.g. BK → Book). Click Remove to delete a mapping, or use the form at the bottom to add a new one.

Material Types

Material types are the item categories patrons choose when submitting a request — for example Book, DVD, or Audiobook. Patrons see these as a dropdown on the submission form.

Click + New Material Type to add one. Click Edit on any row to change the name or toggle it active/inactive. Inactive types won't appear on the patron submission form or in filters.

⚠️
Deleting a material type will remove it from any requests that used it. It's better to set it Inactive to hide it without losing history.

Audiences

Audiences let patrons indicate who the item is for — Adult, Teen, Kids, etc. Like material types, patrons pick from these during submission.

Click + New Audience to add one, or Edit to rename or deactivate an existing audience. Inactive audiences are hidden from the submission form.

Statuses

Statuses are the labels you apply to requests as you process them — for example Pending, On Order, or Purchased. You can customize them to match your workflow.

Status fields

FieldWhat it does
NameThe label shown in the interface, e.g. On Order.
ColorThe badge color used to visually distinguish this status in lists and detail pages. Use the color picker or type a hex code.
DescriptionA plain-language explanation of what this status means. Shown to patrons on the My Requests page so they understand where their request stands.
Sort OrderControls the order statuses appear in dropdowns. Lower numbers appear first.
Notify PatronWhen checked, transitioning a request to this status will trigger a patron email (if notifications are enabled and the patron has an email address). Pair this with an Email Template for that status.
TerminalMark this if the status represents a final state (e.g. Purchased, Denied). Terminal statuses can be used to filter out "closed" requests.
ActiveOnly active statuses appear in the Update Status dropdown. Inactive statuses are hidden from selectors but preserved in history.
💡
Default statuses (Pending, Under Review, On Order, Purchased, Denied, ILL Referred) are seeded automatically with descriptions and Notify Patron enabled. You can rename, recolor, or reorder them freely.

Users

Staff members who log into SFP via Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) are added here automatically the first time they sign in. You don't need to create accounts manually — just have staff log in and then assign their role here.

Roles

RoleAccess
Admin Full access to all requests, patrons, titles, and all Settings pages. Can manage users, groups, statuses, and configuration.
Selector Access to Requests, Patrons, and Titles. Scoped to their assigned Groups — they only see requests that match their group's material types and audiences.

Editing a user

Click Edit on any user to change their role, assign them to selector groups, or toggle their account active/inactive. Setting a user to Inactive prevents them from logging in without deleting their record.

💡
A selector with no group assignments can log in but won't see any requests in their queue. Always assign at least one group after changing a user's role to Selector.

Groups

Groups control which requests each selector sees. A group is a combination of material types and audiences — selectors assigned to a group only see requests that match both.

For example, a group called Adult Books might include the Book material type and the Adult audience. Selectors in that group would only see adult book requests — not DVDs, not teen materials, etc.

Creating or editing a group

  1. Click + New Group (or Edit on an existing one).
  2. Give it a name and optional description.
  3. Check the material types and audiences this group should cover.
  4. Leave Active checked so it appears in user assignment dropdowns.
  5. Click Create Group (or Save Changes).
💡
A selector can belong to multiple groups. Their request queue will show requests matching any of their assigned groups.
⚠️
If you deactivate a group, selectors assigned to it lose that scope from their queue. Reassign them to another group if needed.

Backups

The Backups page provides tools for exporting, importing, and restoring data. It is divided into four areas: Configuration, Database & Storage, Server Backups, and Schedule & Retention.

Configuration

Export or import a JSON snapshot of all non-transient configuration — everything needed to restore a fresh install to the same state, without including patron requests, patrons, or titles. Use this to clone a configuration to another environment or to recover after accidental changes.

The configuration export includes:

What's includedDetails
SettingsAll application key/value configuration pairs.
Request statusesName, color, icon, sort order, action label, SFP/ILL scope, terminal state, patron notification flag, advance-on-claim, and description.
FormsThe SFP and ILL form definitions (used as reference points for field configuration).
FieldsAll form field definitions — type, label, scope (SFP/ILL/both), sort order, required, token inclusion, filterable, and conditional visibility rules. Options for select and radio fields (e.g. material types, audiences, genre, console) are included inline.
Form field configPer-form overrides for each field — visibility, sort order, required state, step assignment, label override, and conditional logic.
Form field option overridesPer-form label, visibility, and order overrides for individual field options.
Selector groupsName, description, active state, and linked material type and audience slugs.
Catalog format labelsBiblioCommons format code → display label mappings.
Staff routing templatesPer-selector-group email subject and body.
Patron status templatesSubject, body, linked statuses, and linked field options (material types) for patron notification emails.
ActionWhat it does
Export ConfigDownloads the full configuration as a .json file.
Import ConfigUpload a previously exported JSON file. Existing records are upserted by slug or name — nothing is deleted. Old backup files that used a legacy format are still supported.
💡
On a fresh install, run migrations and the seeder first, then import a config backup. The import will update seeded defaults with your saved values rather than creating duplicates.

Database & Storage

Full data export and restore tools. These include transient data — requests, patrons, and titles — and should be used for full disaster recovery.

ActionWhat it does
Export DatabaseDownloads a full SQL dump of all tables. MySQL-specific — useful for direct server restores on the same database engine.
Export StorageDownloads a ZIP of the storage/app directory — uploaded files and stored assets. Must be extracted manually to restore.
Import DatabaseRestore the database from an uploaded .sql file. Replaces all existing data.
💡
Scheduled and server-saved database backups use a database-agnostic JSON format that can be restored on any supported driver (MySQL, SQLite, etc.). The Export Database button still produces SQL for direct server-level restores.
⚠️
The Wipe Everything option permanently truncates all tables. You must type WIPE to confirm. This cannot be undone — export a backup first.

Server Backups

Use Save to Server Now to write a config JSON and/or database JSON backup directly to storage/app/requests-backups on the server — no browser download required. Files saved here appear in the Server Backups list below, where you can download, restore, or delete them. Server-saved database backups are in JSON format (database-agnostic).

Schedule & Retention

Set how many days to keep server-side backups before pruning, and use the cron schedule builder to generate a command you can add to your server's crontab. The artisan command php artisan requests:backup accepts --config, --db, --storage, --prune, and --all flags. Database backups written by the command are JSON.
